displayText.simplePara: AMPEL360e Wide Tube-and-Wing Family (eWTW) — All variants

remarks.simplePara: Programme-controlled placeholder. infoCode 040A subject to BREX/SNS/DMRL freeze. Not for operational use.

levelledPara.title: General
levelledPara.para: This data module describes the Cooling System of the AMPEL360e eWTW ECS, aligned to ATLAS node 020-050 and ATA 21-50. The cooling system removes heat from cabin air, avionics equipment and the hybrid-electric propulsion thermal management system, maintaining temperatures within operating limits across all flight phases.

levelledPara.title: Cooling Architecture
levelledPara.para: The eWTW cooling system uses a combination of ram-air heat exchangers and the ECS pack refrigeration cycle to reject heat from conditioned air before it enters the cabin. The hybrid-electric architecture introduces additional heat loads from the power electronics and motor controllers that are integrated into the cooling design.

listItem.para: Primary heat exchangers (ram-air cooled, pack primary and secondary).
Avionics cooling fans and liquid cooling loops.
Power electronics cooling (hybrid-electric variants): liquid cooling circuit rejecting heat to ram air or fuel heat sink.

levelledPara.title: Hybrid-Electric Heat Rejection
levelledPara.para: The eWTW hybrid-electric propulsion system generates significant waste heat from power electronics (inverters, converters, motor controllers) and the electric machines. This heat must be rejected using dedicated cooling circuits that interface with the ECS. The thermal management architecture is shared between the ECS and the hybrid-electric propulsion system and is coordinated by the aircraft energy management controller.

levelledPara.title: Key Components
levelledPara.para: Major components of the cooling subsystem include:

listItem.para: Water separators and moisture management units.
Liquid cooling pumps and heat exchangers (hybrid-electric variants).
